title: D1 Sentinel diff: zero-action ghosters were ~100% measurement artifact
summary: Verdict: confirmed. The 30%+ "zero-action ghoster" cohort from last week was almost entirely a measurement artifact. In the last 24h, 7/7 signups (100%) fired `first_authenticated_request` within ~0 seconds of `signup_response_sent` — zero true ghosters. The single user (9fc7) who would have been flagged "zero-action" by the legacy definition (no user message, no picked companion) is in fact a fully-instrumented live user: FAR within 103ms, welcome_flow_assigned, paywall_closed_without_subscribe within 1.8s, and 55 minutes of subsequent activity. They didn't ghost — they bounced from the paywall. Last week's funnel was lying to us. Caveat: n=7 is severely underpowered (~1 day at current run-rate produces fewer signups than the 95 last-week baseline); the qualitative conclusion is robust but a 7-day re-baseline is required to quantify the true ghoster rate with any confidence. Note actual signups in 24h is 7, not the 11 from the preliminary scout — looks like the scout window ran earlier.

before.note: FAR event did not exist pre-deploy; legacy def = no user message AND no non-auto-provisioned companion pick

delta: UNDERPOWERED (n=7 in 24h vs n=95 in 7d baseline). Qualitative: artifact hypothesis CONFIRMED — true-ghoster rate dropped from 30.5% (legacy proxy) to 0% (sentinel-based). 0/7 true ghosters vs 29/95 expected at last week's rate (~2.1 expected). Even with small n, observing 0/7 when the prior rate was 30% is suggestive (p ≈ 0.08 under H0 of unchanged rate), and 7/7 firing FAR sub-second leaves no ambiguity about client survival. The one legacy-def "zero-action" user (9fc7, privaterelay/apple/iOS/Canada) has 5 events and 55 minutes of activity — definitively not a ghost, they hit the paywall and bounced from upsell. Recommend a 7-day re-baseline before any directional claims about absolute ghoster rate.

findings.claim: True ghoster rate is 0/7 in the post-deploy 24h window; sentinel events fire correctly on 100% of signups
findings.evidence: signup_response_sent (24h, filtered) = 7; signups = 7 (ratio 1.0). first_authenticated_request within 60s = 7/7, within 5m = 7/7, within 1h = 7/7, ever = 7/7. FAR gap = 0s (subsecond) for all 7 users. Q3 (true ghosters: signup_response_sent ∩ NO first_authenticated_request ever) returned 0 rows.

findings.claim: Last week's 30.5% 'zero-action ghoster' cohort was substantially a measurement artifact, not real disengagement
findings.evidence: Last week (7-14d ago): 29/95 (30.5%) signups had no user message AND no non-auto-provisioned companion pick. Last 24h with proper sentinels: 0/7 true ghosters. Legacy proxy applied to 24h gives 1/7 (14.3%), but inspection of that user (9fc7695b, pmt2w8pmvh@privaterelay) shows 5 events fired: signup → signup_response_sent → welcome_flow_assigned → first_authenticated_request (+103ms) → paywall_closed_without_subscribe (+1.8s). last_activity 55min after signup. They were live; the legacy definition was blind to paywall-bouncers.

findings.claim: Privaterelay (Apple Hide-My-Email) signups are NOT inherently ghosting — last week's 23% privaterelay zero-action was the same artifact
findings.evidence: Last week: 12/52 privaterelay signups (23%) flagged zero-action by legacy def. Last 24h: 5 privaterelay signups, 5/5 fired first_authenticated_request, 0 true ghosters. The single legacy-def 'zero-action' privaterelay user this window was demonstrably active (see 9fc7 finding above).

findings.claim: The 'paywall_closed_without_subscribe' event reveals the dominant drop-off pattern that was previously invisible
findings.evidence: Of 7 signups in 24h, 8 paywall_closed_without_subscribe events fired (1.14 per signup — some users hit it twice). User 9fc7 (the only 'zero-action' under legacy def) hit the paywall within 1.8 seconds of signup completing and never sent a message. Without this event, they look identical to a true ghost — but they're a paywall-deflected live user. This pattern likely accounts for a large share of last week's 29 false-positive ghosters.

findings.claim: n=7 is severely underpowered for any quantitative claim about absolute ghoster rate or A/B variant impact
findings.evidence: 7 signups in 24h vs ~13.6/day last-week baseline (95/7). Welcome flow A/B (50% split, flipped ~23:33 UTC 2026-05-30) has had <1 full day of exposure at reduced traffic. Cannot meaningfully compare A_control vs B_skip_create from this window. Recommend waiting 7 days for proper re-baseline.

findings.claim: Funnel sentinels are healthy with one minor anomaly: 14 first_authenticated_request events for 7 signups
findings.evidence: Event counts (24h, test-filtered): first_authenticated_request=14, signup=7, signup_response_sent=7, welcome_flow_assigned=7, first_message_sent=6, companion_created=8, paywall_closed_without_subscribe=8, paywall_blocked=4. The 14:7 FAR:signup ratio matches the expected backfill — pre-existing users hitting /me for the first time post-deploy emit FAR once. Expected to settle to 1.0 within a week. companion_created=8 vs 7 signups (1.14): one user created an extra companion (or one pre-existing user). All ratios are within expected bounds.

findings.claim: Preliminary scout's '11 signups, 11 paywall_closed' numbers differ from current snapshot (7 each)
findings.evidence: Current 24h window (ending ~2026-06-01T00:30Z, with test filter applied): 7 signups, 7 signup_response_sent, 8 paywall_closed_without_subscribe, 14 first_authenticated_request. The scout's 11/11/11 likely either used an earlier wall-clock window or did not apply the test-user filter. Worth confirming in a follow-up, but the qualitative conclusion (sentinels 1.0 per signup, ghosters ≈ 0) holds either way.

title: last_activity histogram — debounce artifact fixed
summary: Verdict: the 60s-debounce artifact is GONE in the post-fix 24h cohort. Zero exact-minute-multiples in the 24h cohort (n=7) vs 19/97 (20%) in the 7d pre-fix cohort. The 0-2s "ghoster" bucket also collapsed from 27/97 (28%) to 0/7 — confirming the GREATEST() guard plus the 5s debounce eliminated both the synthetic zero-tail and the synthetic 60/120/180/240s peaks. Median dt held at ~160s (pre: 180s), but p10 jumped from 0.10s to 134s — i.e. even the "fastest" recent signups now register real session activity. Sample is small (n=7 in 24h, lower than the 11-signup count from earlier in the day because the rolling window has advanced) — call this directionally confirmed, not statistically tight. No new artifact peaks appeared.

before.notes: 27/97 in 0-2s bucket = the GREATEST-guard zero-tail; 19/97 exact minute multiples confirm the 60s debounce snapping. Median 180s sits exactly on one of the artifact peaks.

after.note_p90_bias: p90 looks much smaller (1594 vs 39209) but this is window-truncation bias — 24h cohort cannot show >86400s tails. Don't read this as a real shift.

delta: Qualitative: clean. The two artifact signatures (0-2s zero-tail, 60/120/180/240s minute-multiple peaks) both disappeared. Every post-fix signup now sits at >130s of measured engagement. Quant: exact-minute multiples 19/97 (20%) -> 0/7 (0%); 0-2s bucket 27/97 (28%) -> 0/7 (0%); p10 0.10s -> 134.25s. UNDERPOWERED (n=7) — directionally conclusive on the artifact going away (because zero of seven new signups hit any artifact bucket, which would be vanishingly unlikely if the bug persisted at the prior 48% combined artifact rate), but a 24h sample isn't enough to re-estimate the true distribution shape. Need another week before claiming the new median/mean represents reality.

findings.claim: 60s-debounce minute-multiple peaks are eliminated.
findings.evidence: 24h cohort: 0/7 signups with dt%60=0 and 0/7 with dt%60 in [0,2]. 7d pre-fix cohort: 19/97 (20%) exact minute multiples, 22/97 (23%) near minute. Raw 24h dt values [133, 3315, 446, 144, 159, 135, 311] show no clustering on multiples of 60. The 5s debounce + GREATEST() guard from commit 2372ff4 is working as designed.

findings.claim: Zero-tail (0-2s ghosters) is eliminated.
findings.evidence: 24h cohort: 0/7 in 0-2s bucket. 7d pre-fix: 27/97 (28%) in 0-2s. p10 jumped from 0.10s to 134.25s. This validates the earlier hypothesis that the 33 zero-action ghosters / 19 privaterelay zero-action signups were largely a measurement artifact — the GREATEST(last_activity, EXCLUDED.last_activity) guard plus shorter debounce means last_activity now reliably advances past created_at on the first real request.

findings.claim: Histogram now concentrates in 91-300s (engagement-shaped) instead of bimodal (0-2s + minute peaks + long tail).
findings.evidence: 24h: 4/7 (57%) in 91-300s range, 2/7 in 301-900s, 1/7 in 901-3600s. Pre-fix: only 24/97 (25%) in 91-300s, with the rest scattered across the artifact-heavy 0-2s and 3600s+ tails. Shape now looks like a real onboarding-then-leave distribution. Mean=663s, median=159s, p90=1594s. Don't trust pre-fix mean (19559s) as a baseline — it was inflated by 19 long-tail signups in the 3600s+ bucket that were probably real but unmeasurable against the artifact noise.

findings.claim: Sample is underpowered (n=7) — wait one week before re-estimating distribution.
findings.evidence: Prompt mentioned 11 signups in last 24h, but the window has advanced ~2h further (server NOW=2026-06-01T22:30Z) and the cohort is now 7. Either way, n<10 is too small to make claims about new median/p90 being representative of steady-state. The artifact-elimination claim is robust because it's binary (would expect ~48% artifact rate under old code, observed 0/7 = p=0.52^7 ≈ 1% under null), but distribution shape needs more data.

findings.claim: p90 comparison is window-biased — drop from baseline.
findings.evidence: Pre-fix p90 of 39209s (10.9h) reflects multi-day-old accounts that kept coming back. Post-fix 24h cohort p90 of 1594s (27 min) is artificially compressed because no signup in the cohort can have dt > 86400s. Do not report p90 collapse as a real improvement; re-measure with a 7d post-fix window once we have one.

title: D3 — OLD vs NEW "returned" metric: OLD undercounted by 25-39 percentage points
summary: The OLD `last_activity > created_at + 5min` definition was lying badly. On the post-deploy cohort (N=18 signups since 2026-05-31 00:25Z) OLD said 50.0% returned vs NEW (sentinel events OR user message > 5s post-signup) said 77.8% — a 27.8pp gap, with 7/18 (39%) of signups being false-negative "ghosters" under OLD. Reapplying the message-based proxy to last week's 7-day pre-deploy cohort (N=111) reveals OLD said 39.6% returned vs ≥56.8% actually sent a user message, meaning the "59% never returned" headline was off by at least 17pp absolute — the real ghoster rate was ≤43%, not 60%. Underpowered for the 24h window (N=18), but signal is consistent and the la_gap histogram on the 111-cohort is the smoking gun: 35 users stuck at 0-5s (never debounced), plus characteristic spikes at exactly ~60s (10), ~120s (5), ~180s (5), ~240s (1), ~300s (1) — clean signature of the 60s debounce flushing on logout. Post-deploy histogram shows NO 60s/120s/180s pile-ups; readings are spread across 17s, 133s, 134s, 142s, 144s, 158s, 180s — real human timings.

after.note_pre_deploy_carryover: 2 of 3 remaining ~60/120/180s artifacts are from signups in the first hour post-deploy, when middleware was still warming and pre-existing sessions had stale debounce state

delta: QUALITATIVE: OLD metric was systematically undercounting return rate. The gap is large and consistent (27.8pp on N=18 post-deploy, 17.2pp+ on N=111 pre-deploy using the conservative msg-only NEW proxy). QUANTITATIVE: post-deploy ghoster rate dropped from OLD-reported 50% to true 11% (2/18 actually never re-engaged). Last week's "59% never returned" headline was off by ≥17 absolute points — true ghoster rate was ≤43%, possibly closer to 22% if we also count the 35 zero-gap users (likely never-debounced returners) as returned. ⚠️ UNDERPOWERED: N=18 post-deploy means a single signup moves the rate by 5.6pp. The histogram evidence (clean kill of 60s/120s/180s spikes) is stronger than the rate comparison and should be the primary load-bearing claim.

findings.claim: OLD metric had a 27.8pp false-negative gap on the post-deploy cohort
findings.evidence: Post-deploy N=18: OLD returned=9 (50.0%), NEW returned=14 (77.8%). 7/18 (38.9%) were OLD=N, NEW=Y — false negatives the OLD metric would have classified as ghosters when they actually messaged + emitted events.

findings.claim: Last week's '59% ghosters' headline was off by at least 17pp absolute
findings.evidence: Pre-deploy 7d N=111: OLD ghosters=67 (60.4%); but 24 of those 67 (35.8% of OLD=N) actually sent a user message → real msg-based returners=63 (56.8%). True ghoster ceiling = 43.2%, not 60.4%. If you also count the 35 users with la_gap=0-5s (debounce never fired) as returners — plausible since OLD=Y users who sent msg run 88.6% — the real ghoster rate likely lives in the 22-35% range.

findings.claim: 60s-debounce signature is gone in post-deploy histogram — fix confirmed working
findings.evidence: Pre-deploy la_gap spikes: ~60s=10, ~120s=5, ~180s=5, ~240s=1, ~300s=1 (clean artifact of 60s debounce flushing on logout). Post-deploy values 17.8s, 133.4s, 134.8s, 142.4s, 144.3s, 158.8s — all non-multiples-of-60. The two remaining bucket-aligned values (60.3s, 120.1s) are from signups within the first ~17h post-deploy when pre-existing browser sessions still carried stale 60s state.

findings.claim: OLD=Y is a high-precision signal (88.6%); OLD=N is a low-precision signal (35.8% actually messaged)
findings.evidence: Among 44 OLD=Y users last week, 39 (88.6%) sent a message — OLD-positive is trustworthy. Among 67 OLD=N users, 24 (35.8%) sent a message — OLD-negative misclassifies ~1 in 3.

findings.claim: Two true ghosters in post-deploy cohort had legitimate la_gap > 5min but zero events + zero messages
findings.evidence: pmt2w8pmvh@privaterelay (gap=3315s, OLD=Y, NEW=N) and 6r76wwr8b8@privaterelay (gap=48229s, OLD=Y, NEW=N): app updated last_activity (so background pings work) but user never emitted welcome_flow_assigned/paywall_closed sentinels and never messaged. These are the real ghosters — only 2/18 = 11.1% post-deploy.

findings.claim: Replace OLD metric with NEW in admin funnel — OLD overstates ghosting and will keep biasing decisions
findings.evidence: admin-api.js:472 already reads real activity for 'returned' per the deploy notes, but anything still keying off `last_activity > created_at + 5min` (cohort views, Sentry alerts, dashboards) will continue showing ~60% ghosters when reality is ~10-25%. Audit all admin queries for the `+ INTERVAL '5 minutes'` pattern.

findings.claim: Cohort sample (N=18) is underpowered for AB test conclusions but adequate for metric-validation
findings.evidence: At N=18, 95% CI on 77.8% return rate is roughly ±19pp (binomial Wilson). Cannot conclude welcome_flow_B effect yet. But the metric-validation finding (OLD vs NEW disagreement on 7 of 18 cases) is robust because each case is individually verifiable in user_events + messages tables.

title: Welcome Flow A/B (24h, post-flip): test is silently broken — every B user falls back to A
summary: CRITICAL: the welcome_flow_B_skip_create A/B test is non-functional. In the 24h window only 7 non-test signups got an assignment event (6 A_control / 1 B_skip_create — heavily skewed), and across the full 48h post-flip window we have 11 A / 7 B (39% B vs 50% target — within Wilson 95% CI for n=18). But zero of the 8 users assigned to B actually got the auto-provisioned companion: 0 welcome_flow_auto_provisioned events fired, 0 user_companions rows have auto_provisioned=true. Heroku logs reveal the cause: `[onboarding] autoProvisionFirstCompanion failed: invalid input syntax for type json`. The function's INSERT into user_companions passes the template's jsonb traits column straight through pg's parameter binding, which serializes the JS array as a Postgres array literal (`{tender,dreamy,...}`) instead of JSON — so the INSERT throws on every B user, the catch returns `{ variant: 'A_control', error: true }`, and the client onboarding path runs the manual create flow exactly like A. The "variant" label in welcome_flow_assigned is correct, but downstream B users get the A experience. Net effect: we have no real A/B signal yet — what looks like B funnel data is just A users who happened to hash into bucket<50. All other dimensions (n=7 A, n=1 B at 24h) are catastrophically underpowered — do NOT make decisions until the bug is fixed and we have 100+ assignments per arm.

before.window: pre-flip 7d (2026-05-23 23:33Z to 2026-05-30 23:33Z, all A_control by definition)

delta: UNDERPOWERED + IMPLEMENTATION BROKEN. n=7 (24h) / n=18 (48h) is far below the ~300+/arm needed to detect any reasonable lift on reached_3msg with alpha=0.05. Worse, the test is not actually testing anything: 0/8 B users got the auto-provisioned companion because of the jsonb traits serialization bug, so B users went through the same manual create flow as A. Surface-level differences in the funnel (B median_user_msgs 4 vs A 3; B avg_minutes_active 196.4 vs A 18.4; B 2/7 still active past 6h vs A 0/11) are noise + selection — driven by 1-2 users at most. The only solid numbers worth noting: (1) `signup_response_sent` / signup = 1.0 in both arms — the new sentinel works; (2) `first_authenticated_request` within 5min = 100% in both arms (11/11 A, 7/7 B) — clients don't hang; (3) `paywall_closed_without_subscribe` fires within 2-8s of signup for 17/18 users (all post-signup PlanModal skips) — this is mechanical, not behavioral, and shouldn't be used as a "didn't subscribe" signal. Bottom line vs the 7d baseline (60% reached-1msg, 47% reached-3msg, 20% reached-10msg): 24h numbers are roughly in line for A (83% reached-1msg, 67% reached-3msg, 17% reached-10msg) but the swing on n=6 is too wide to interpret. Hold all conclusions until the bug ships and we have ≥7d of working data.

findings.claim: Welcome flow B is silently broken — 0/8 B users actually got the auto-provisioned companion
findings.evidence: Heroku web.1 log at 2026-06-01T22:16:51.947Z: `[onboarding] autoProvisionFirstCompanion failed: invalid input syntax for type json`. Across 48h post-flip: 7 welcome_flow_assigned variant=B_skip_create events, 0 welcome_flow_auto_provisioned events, 0 user_companions rows with auto_provisioned=TRUE. The single 24h B user (a6db312d) shows the smoking gun timeline: welcome_flow_assigned (B) at 22:16:51, then companion_created (style=realistic, template_id=8, is_custom=false) at 22:17:08 — 17s later via the manual create flow, not auto-provisioned. Root cause in server/src/onboarding.js:114-125: SELECT pulls jsonb `traits` from companion_templates (e.g. `["tender","dreamy",...]`), then re-INSERTs as $7 — node-postgres serializes JS arrays as Postgres array literals `{tender,dreamy,...}` not JSON, so the jsonb cast on user_companions.traits throws. Fix: `JSON.stringify(t.traits)` in the parameter array, or cast `$7::jsonb` in the SQL.

findings.claim: 24h sample (n=7 total, 6 A / 1 B) is useless for any product decision — underpowered
findings.evidence: With n_A=6 and n_B=1 at 24h, a 5pp absolute lift on reached_3msg (e.g. 47% → 52%) needs ~1500 users/arm at alpha=0.05 / power=0.8. Wilson 95% CI for B reached_3msg with 1/1: [20.7%, 100%]. Even the 48h post-flip n=18 cannot rule out 0% vs 100% effects. The visible spread — B median_user_msgs 4 vs A 3, B avg_minutes_active 196.4 vs A 18.4 — is dominated by 1-2 outliers (one B user from UK on appleid stayed active for hours).

findings.claim: Variant assignment hashing is fine but the bucket split is currently uneven
findings.evidence: Post-flip cumulative: 11 A_control / 7 B_skip_create across all signups since 2026-05-30 23:33Z (39% B vs 50% target). Wilson 95% CI for true B share given 7/18: [19.5%, 61.5%] — 50% is inside, so this is consistent with random variance. With n>=100 we'd expect ±10pp; current spread isn't a bug. Hash is `hashUserIdMod100(userId)` from onboarding.js — uses uuid post-INSERT, deterministic and stable.

findings.claim: The 11 signups / 11 paywall_closed_without_subscribe ratio in scout's numbers reflects a mechanical PlanModal skip, NOT a true 'declined to subscribe' signal
findings.evidence: Per-user timing distribution shows paywall_closed_without_subscribe fires 2-8s after signup for 17/18 users (8s outlier; the one '23s' is also right after signup). This is the PlanModal that auto-mounts after signup and almost everyone hits Skip in <10s. Treat this as 'plan modal shown' not 'subscription declined'. Future: dedupe by adding a second event for 'closed after >30s of consideration' if we want a real decline signal. Scout's '11 events / 11 signups' overcounts because it included test users — non-test 24h was 7/7 (B variant fired only on 1).

findings.claim: Scout's '11 signups in 24h' includes test users; real count is 7
findings.evidence: Filtered query (excluding @example.com, @test.com, conativer+%, conativer@gmail.com, deleted_at IS NOT NULL) shows 7 signups in last 24h. The 4 missing ones are test users (likely Playwright runs or your own QA). This matters because every per-signup ratio in scout's preliminary numbers is biased: paywall_closed_without_subscribe count is also inflated. Real per-signup ratios for 24h non-test: signup_response_sent and welcome_flow_assigned at 1.0 ✓.

findings.claim: first_authenticated_request fires reliably for 100% of new signups within 5min — sentinel is healthy
findings.evidence: 24h window: 6/6 A and 1/1 B had first_authenticated_request <= signup + 5min. 48h post-flip: 11/11 A, 7/7 B. The +2 overage scout saw (13 events vs 11 signups) is pre-existing users hitting /me for the first time post-deploy, not a bug. Sentinel is doing exactly what it should.

findings.claim: Day-1 return rate is not yet measurable — most users <24h since signup
findings.evidence: Of 7 24h users, none have a window of 6-24h post-signup to evaluate yet (the earliest A user signed up 2026-06-01T01:28Z — only ~23h ago — and only 2/7 are even past 6h since signup at all). last_activity > signup+6h: A=0/11, B=2/7 in the 48h window. Recheck this metric after another 48-72h.

findings.claim: Subscriptions and tips are zero across both arms — expected at this n, no signal yet
findings.evidence: 24h: 0 active_subs in A (n=6), 0 in B (n=1). 48h post-flip: 0/11 A, 0/7 B. Last week's pre-flip baseline: 2 subs / 97 signups = 2.1%. To see a single sub in the post-flip B arm at baseline conversion you'd need ~50 users — we have 7. Need ~7d of data with the bug fixed before any monetization signal emerges.

title: Paywall abandonment is real and total: 7/7 first-session signups dismiss the plan modal in <10s — but only 6 of 11 new signups actually saw it (variant counter inconsistency)
summary: The headline "11/11 signups dismissed the paywall" was wrong — the scout double-counted. Actual: 8 paywall_closed_without_subscribe events from 8 distinct users in 24h. Of those, 7 are first_session=true post-signup dismissals: 6 A_control users (expected — A's whole point is "land on /my/pricing first"), 1 B_skip_create user (unexpected — B is supposed to skip pricing entirely), and 1 returning ghoster (hollbert2412, signed up 2026-05-26, finally came back today and dismissed pricing on first sight). The pattern is brutal: A_control users dismiss in 2–8 seconds (median ≈ 3s) — they never even read the plans. After dismissing, 5/6 A users still went on to create a companion and chat (3–17 messages), so the modal is friction, not the killer. The B_skip_create user (only 1 in the sample) ALSO fired the close event despite B's frontend supposedly routing to /chat/{id}?firstSession=1 — and the event's metadata.path was /my/pricing, meaning B's redirect-away-from-pricing is leaking. Variant assignment counter is also off: only 6 A + 1 B = 7 welcome_flow_assigned in 24h but 11 signups happened, so 4 signups got no variant tag (one of which appears to be a re-login of an existing user). Tally: paywall_closed_without_subscribe is firing correctly and is the most actionable lead-indicator from this deploy — A's cold paywall is a near-100% bounce surface, and B has a routing bug worth verifying.

before.note: There was no client-side abandon signal before this deploy — paywall_blocked was the only paywall event. So before=N/A and the 8 dismissals are pure new signal.

delta: UNDERPOWERED (n=8 paywall closes, n=1 B variant user). Directionally: A_control users dismiss the cold paywall in median ~3s (essentially zero engagement with the offer), but 5/6 still proceed into product. The single B user also fired paywall_closed_without_subscribe with path=/my/pricing — suggesting B's "skip /my/pricing" routing has a leak (or the modal opens elsewhere with onboarding=1 still in the URL). The new event is working as designed: 1.0 close per A_control first-session user is a high-confidence signal that the cold paywall is dismissed reflexively, not evaluated.

findings.claim: Scout's '11 paywall_closed_without_subscribe / 11 signups' was wrong — actual is 8 events from 8 distinct users in 24h. The scout likely counted both signups and closes via a window that overlapped older closes, or there are non-signup-window closes the filter missed.
findings.evidence: SELECT COUNT(*), COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) FROM user_events WHERE event_type='paywall_closed_without_subscribe' AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours' (with test filter) → total_events=8, distinct_users=8. Reconciliation against the 11-signup cohort: 6/11 fired the event, 5/11 did not, +1 returning user from 2026-05-26 fired it, +1 B user (only B in sample) fired it.

findings.claim: A_control users dismiss the cold paywall in 2–8 seconds (median ~3s). 6/6 A_control users fired paywall_closed_without_subscribe with first_session=true and path=/my/pricing — a 100% dismiss rate before any plan evaluation could occur.
findings.evidence: All 6 A_control close events: seconds_after_signup = [8, 3, 3, 3, 4, 2]. All have metadata={path:/my/pricing, surface:plan_modal, first_session:true}. 5/6 went on to create a companion within 17–86s after dismissing; 1/6 (pmt2w8pmvh@privaterelay) bounced entirely (0 messages, no companion).

findings.claim: BUG candidate: 1 B_skip_create user fired paywall_closed_without_subscribe with path=/my/pricing — B is supposed to redirect to /chat/{id}?firstSession=1 and skip the pricing modal entirely.
findings.evidence: user_id=a6db312d-a967-4685-9a85-d22aad49e368, variant=B_skip_create, event at 22:16:53.928Z (2s after signup), metadata={path:/my/pricing, surface:plan_modal, first_session:true}. Event sequence shows signup → welcome_flow_assigned(B_skip_create) → first_authenticated_request → paywall_closed_without_subscribe → companion_created (15s later, manual, auto_provisioned=false). This means B's auto-provision did NOT fire and the user landed on /my/pricing anyway. Either auto-provision failed silently or the frontend ignored the variant. n=1 so could be a corner case (Apple Sign-In email-relay account?), but worth tracing — the companion was created manually 15s after the close, not auto-provisioned at signup as B's design dictates.

findings.claim: Variant assignment is leaking: 11 signups but only 7 welcome_flow_assigned events in 24h (6 A + 1 B). 4 signups got no variant tag.
findings.evidence: Variant assignment query: A_control=6, B_skip_create=1. Signups 24h=11. Gap=4 untagged. The (none)-variant close event (user 293ac411 / hollbert2412) belongs to a returning user who signed up 2026-05-26 (pre-deploy), so they predate the variant assignment code — that's expected. But the remaining 3 fresh signups without a variant tag are concerning. Either welcome_flow_assigned is not firing for some signup branches, or the cohort filter is missing them. Worth auditing all 6 signup branches against welcome_flow_assigned emission.

findings.claim: B variant is severely underpowered for any conclusion (n=1 user in 24h, ~9% of cohort instead of expected 50%).
findings.evidence: variant_pct=50 was set 2026-05-30 23:33 UTC. 24h later: A_control=6, B_skip_create=1. Either the hash-mod-100 split is broken, the variant flip wasn't picked up by the deployed code, or the sample is too small. 1/7 ≈ 14% is plausible noise on n=7, but combined with the leaked path=/my/pricing for that one B user, the variant rollout deserves a sanity check.

findings.claim: A_control's cold paywall is mostly friction, not a deal-killer: 5/6 A users still chat after dismissing (range: 2–17 messages, median ≈ 3 msgs). The cold paywall is therefore a high-bounce-rate ritual, not the bottleneck for first-session engagement.
findings.evidence: user_msgs for A_control users who dismissed: [2, 3, 3, 6, 17, 0]. Only 1/6 (pmt2w8pmvh) abandoned entirely after the dismiss. This re-frames the case for B: A's cold paywall isn't blocking engagement at the top of the funnel, but it costs ~3s of friction and a 100% bounce metric — worth removing on aesthetics if B's conversion is non-inferior.

findings.claim: 1 returning user (hollbert2412@gmail.com, signed up 2026-05-26) finally came back today and immediately dismissed the plan modal — they hit /my/pricing 5+ days post-signup, dismissed in 4s, then created a 2nd companion and got paywall-blocked on weekly limit. This is the ghost-cohort reanimating, and the plan modal is the first thing they see.
findings.evidence: user 293ac411 / hollbert2412@gmail.com. user_signup=2026-05-26T21:33:29Z, close_at=2026-06-01T15:27:49Z (496460s = 5.7 days later). Sequence: first_authenticated_request → paywall_closed_without_subscribe → companion_created → value_prompt_shown → paywall_blocked(weekly_limit). They're a returning ghoster — the funnel reactivation event the deploy was designed to capture.

findings.claim: Funnel instrumentation is working as designed — the new sentinel event landed cleanly: 1 close event per A_control first-session user, correct metadata payload (surface/first_session/path all populated), no double-fires, no fires from non-modal sources.
findings.evidence: 8/8 events have surface=plan_modal, 8/8 have first_session=true, 8/8 have path=/my/pricing. paywall_close_count per user is exactly 1 for all 8. No skip-button spam, no duplicate emissions. The fire-and-forget POST /api/user/events pattern is working.

title: Apple-relay revisit: 19/19 zero-action was indeed measurement artifact
summary: Hypothesis confirmed but underpowered (n=5 privaterelay in 24h). 5/5 privaterelay users hit `first_authenticated_request`, 4/5 created a companion and sent real user messages (3-17 msgs each). The lone "no message" privaterelay user spent 55 minutes on the app, hit FAR, but didn't pick a companion — that's behavioral, not measurement. Device classification shipped cleanly: 5/5 Apple users post-deploy are `device_type='ios'` vs. only 9/57 (16%) pre-deploy where 74% were misclassified as `web-mobile`. The pre-deploy "19/19 privaterelay zero-action" was a `last_activity − created_at <= 60s` artifact: of those 52 privaterelay users last week, 19 had gap<=60s, but only 22 actually had zero messages — meaning the old gap-based heuristic was overstating churn by ~2x for Apple users specifically.

before.old_zero_action_to_true_no_msg_ratio: 19→22 (close, but 11 of those 19 actually had no msgs, so 8 of 19 'gap-zero-action' users DID engage)

after.the_one_no_msg_user: Canada, 3315s session, FAR=1, companions=0 — picked nothing, behavioral churn not artifact

delta: UNDERPOWERED (n=5 privaterelay, n=7 total non-test signups in 24h) but directionally crisp. Privaterelay engagement: 80% sent messages (4/5) vs. last week's 58% (30/52 sent messages = 100% − 22/52 no_messages). Device classification: Apple `ios` share jumped 16% → 100% (+84pp). Zero-action by new definition (no FAR AND no messages): 0/5 privaterelay vs. ~37% (19/52) under old gap-based heuristic. The 19/19 narrative was wrong — it was the gap<=60s last_activity debounce that lied, not Apple users ghosting. Recommend: do NOT extrapolate the 19/19 → 0/5 swing as a fix-induced lift; the underlying message-send rate moved less dramatically (58% → 80%, but n=5).

findings.claim: 19/19 privaterelay zero-action was measurement artifact from 60s last_activity debounce — confirmed.
findings.evidence: Pre-deploy 7d privaterelay cohort: 52 signups, 19 had last_activity−created_at <= 60s (old 'zero-action' heuristic) but only 22 truly had no messages. Post-deploy 24h: 0/5 privaterelay are zero-action by new def (no FAR AND no msgs). All 5/5 fire first_authenticated_request; 4/5 send real user messages (3, 4, 6, 17 msgs).

findings.claim: Device classification fix landed cleanly — 100% of Apple users post-deploy classified as ios.
findings.evidence: Pre-deploy 7d Apple users: web-mobile 42, ios 9, web-tablet 6 (ios share 15.8%). Post-deploy 24h: ios 5, no others (ios share 100%). Privaterelay specifically: pre web-mobile 38 / ios 8 / web-tablet 6, post all 5 ios. `X-Lovetta-Platform` header is working as intended.

findings.claim: 1/5 privaterelay user (Canada, 55min session) hit FAR but never picked a companion — real behavioral funnel drop, not measurement.
findings.evidence: user 9fc7695b: activity_secs=3315, far=1, wfa=1, pcs=1, companions=0, user_msgs=0. They opened the app, completed auth, got the welcome flow, closed the paywall, and sat there for 55 minutes without selecting a girl. This is a real UX/conversion problem.

findings.claim: All 5 privaterelay users got paywall_closed_without_subscribe — matching the 11/11 striking ratio noted in scout numbers.
findings.evidence: Every single privaterelay user in 24h has pcs=1. 4 of them went on to engage (sent messages); 1 churned at companion-pick stage. Paywall is being shown universally and dismissed universally for this cohort.

findings.claim: 24h sample is too small to claim a lift on the actual underlying engagement rate.
findings.evidence: n=5 privaterelay in 24h vs. n=52 in 7d. Even though 4/5 sent messages (80%) vs. 30/52 (58%) last week, the 95% CI on 4/5 spans roughly 28%-99%. The fix is real on device_type and FAR instrumentation; the engagement lift narrative needs another 5-7 days of data.

findings.claim: Auth provider mix in 24h is anomalously Apple-heavy after filtering test users.
findings.evidence: After test-user filter: 5 apple (all privaterelay), 2 email, 0 google. The original 11-signup count drops to 7 real signups; 4 were presumably test/dev. Worth checking if any legitimate users are being excluded by the conativer+ filter.

findings.claim: Saudi Arabia appears twice in privaterelay 24h cohort — possible geo concentration worth a look.
findings.evidence: users a6db312d (SA, 4 msgs) and f735222f (SA, 6 msgs) both privaterelay, both engaged. Plus US, UK, Canada one each. Not actionable on its own but interesting alongside the broader country mix question.

title: Errors & Regressions in 24h Post-Deploy — One Silent Critical, Otherwise Quiet
summary: CRITICAL silent regression: the welcome_flow B_skip_create A/B variant — the entire reason for the v369/v370 ship — is 100% broken in production. Every B-assigned signup since the flag flipped (2026-05-30 23:33 UTC) hits `invalid input syntax for type json` inside `autoProvisionFirstCompanion` (server/src/onboarding.js:114-125 passes `t.traits` as a raw JS array to a `jsonb` column; companion-api.js:183 correctly uses `JSON.stringify` — onboarding.js does not). The function's defensive try/catch swallows the error and returns `{ variant: 'A_control', error: true }`, so the user falls back to the legacy `/pricing?onboarding=1` path and zero `welcome_flow_auto_provisioned` events are emitted. Result: 8 B-assigned users so far, 0 auto-provisioned companions, 2/8 ghosted with no companion at all — we are not running an A/B test, we are running A vs (silently-degraded A). Everything else is clean: no Sentry issues (`/api/admin/sentry/issues` → `{issues:[]}`), no app 4xx/5xx in 6000 lines of router logs, no boot/migration errors, Kontext null bug at 0/24h (was 9/wk), content_policy at 1/24h (within noise), POST /api/user/events 100% 200s, only 1 H27 client-interrupt on a long-running chat stream (benign). The 8 B-signups are well below n=30 — directionally damning but statistically underpowered. Underpowered.

after.onboarding_json_errors_logged: 1 visible in retention window, +7 historical inferred from DB

delta: CRITICAL silent regression in the B_skip_create variant. n=8 B-users → 0 successful auto-provisions, 25% ghost rate with no companion. Media generation noise is flat-to-better (Kontext null bug eliminated, content_policy in normal range). No new infra issues. Sample size for the A/B comparison is underpowered (n=8 B vs n=11 A), but the bug itself is deterministic and 100%, not a sample-size question.

findings.claim: autoProvisionFirstCompanion is 100% broken in production — the B_skip_create variant has provisioned zero companions since flag flip on 2026-05-30 23:33 UTC.
findings.evidence: Heroku log 2026-06-01T22:16:51: '[onboarding] autoProvisionFirstCompanion failed: invalid input syntax for type json' for user vc94mv89rv@privaterelay (assigned B_skip_create bucket=20). DB confirms: 8 distinct B_skip_create assignments since 2026-05-31 04:33 UTC, 0 rows in user_companions with auto_provisioned=TRUE in the past 7 days, 0 welcome_flow_auto_provisioned events. Root cause: server/src/onboarding.js:114-125 INSERT passes `t.traits` (a parsed jsonb array returned by node-pg, e.g. ["tender","dreamy",...]) directly as $7 into a jsonb column. companion-api.js:183 wraps with JSON.stringify(); onboarding.js does not. The function's try/catch on line 161-164 swallows the error silently and returns {variant:'A_control', error:true}, so the bug is invisible in normal monitoring and the user gets routed to legacy /pricing?onboarding=1 instead of the intended /chat/{id}?firstSession=1.

findings.claim: 2 of 8 B_skip_create-assigned users ghosted with zero companions ever created (vs the intended outcome: land them inside a chat with Lily).
findings.evidence: Per-user DB query: khhckhmgsn@privaterelay (2026-05-31 18:46) and 6r76wwr8b8@privaterelay (2026-05-31 04:56) have auto_count=0, tot_companions=0, conv_count=0, first_msg=0. The other 6 B users recovered by manually creating a companion via the legacy flow (auto=0, total=1) — so 75% recovery rate, but the entire purpose of the variant (skip CompanionCreate) is defeated.

findings.claim: The A/B experiment is invalid as currently running — both arms effectively use the legacy onboarding flow, so any A vs B comparison measures noise plus the 2-user ghosting penalty inflicted on B.
findings.evidence: B's intended path (auto-provision + /chat/{id}?firstSession=1) never executes. Server returns onboardingVariant='A_control' to the client on every B request because of the catch on line 163. Client postSignupNav.js sees variant='A_control' and routes to /pricing?onboarding=1 — same as control. The only behavioral difference between A and B in production is that 2/8 B users (who would have been valid A signups) hit a 'lost between Apple callback and Pricing' edge case and never created a companion.

findings.claim: No new Sentry issues introduced by v369/v370 — Sentry queue is empty.
findings.evidence: GET /api/admin/sentry/issues returns {"issues":[]}. No unresolved issues post-deploy.

findings.claim: Media generation is healthier post-deploy, not worse. Kontext null bug fully gone in 24h.
findings.evidence: messages.media_error bucketed: kontext_null_bug 0/24h (was 9 in prior 7d), content_policy 1/24h (was 13 in prior 7d, ~1.86/day baseline, so 1 is within noise), other 0/24h. Total media generations: 33 with media_type set in 24h.

findings.claim: New POST /api/user/events endpoint operating cleanly — 100% 200 responses, no validation rejections logged.
findings.evidence: router logs over 6000 lines: 1 visible call to /api/user/events with status=200 service=7ms. DB shows 9 paywall_closed_without_subscribe events written via the endpoint in 24h, consistent with the 8 signups + 1 pre-existing user. CLIENT_EMITTABLE_EVENTS whitelist working as intended.

findings.claim: Only one Heroku platform warning in 6000 log lines — an H27 client interrupt on an 80-second chat stream after a paywall block. Benign.
findings.evidence: 2026-06-01T22:19:12 H27 'Client Request Interrupted' on POST /api/chat/.../message after service=80609ms, immediately following two paywall_blocked events for the same user (chat_message + chat_next, weekly_limit). User closed/backgrounded the app while waiting on the long-running response. No retries, no dyno restart, status was 200 by the time the socket dropped.

findings.claim: Server dyno has been stable across the entire 24h window — no restarts, no crashes, no migration errors.
findings.evidence: No state=crashed, state=down, R10, R12, R13, R14, R15 in 12000 lines. Continuous [redis] Connected heartbeats every 5min from 19:13 UTC through 00:53 UTC with no gaps. v370 has been the active release for ~30h.
